title: 'A great one he is': Trump wishes Modi on record tenure; PM pitches ties with mutual benefits
content: US President Donald Trump congratulated Prime Minister Narendra Modi on becoming India's longest-serving elected Prime Minister. Trump described Modi as a "strong, healthy, and wise man" and predicted many years of "greatness and success" for him.

title: Government summons top US diplomat over Oman ship attack that left 3 Indians missing
content: India summoned a US diplomat to protest the attack on the commercial vessel Settebello off Oman's coast, which carried 24 Indian crew. While 21 have been rescued, three remain missing. New Delhi is urging de-escalation and the restoration of safe navigation in international waters following the incident.

title: Stokes dropped, Joe Root returns as Test captain as nightclub row rocks England
content: Stokes has been left out of the squad along with fast bowler Gus Atkinson after the pair breached the team's midnight curfew in the early hours of Monday.

title: AI windfall for the public? Trump signals shake-up for tech giants
content: US President Trump is pushing for major AI firms to "give back" to the public, potentially through government stakes or benefit mechanisms. He plans to meet top tech executives soon to discuss this proposal, aiming to enrich citizens and boost AI's popularity. Discussions about a "Public Wealth Fund" model, distributing AI investment returns, are reportedly already underway.

title: Bill Gates warns Microsoft, Amazon, Google on data center push
content: Bill Gates has told the AI industry it does not have permission to drive up household electricity bills, warning Amazon, Google, Meta and Microsoft that the old utility-funded grid model is finished. Speaking on CNBC, the Microsoft co-founder said hyperscalers must now pick data centre sites where the economics and politics hold. With 48 projects worth $156 billion already blocked in 2025 and public opposition at record highs, the buildout is colliding with sentiment.

title: EU orders Meta to give OpenAI and other AI rivals free access to WhatsApp
content: EU antitrust regulators have ordered Meta to grant rival AI companies free access to WhatsApp's business API. This decision comes amid an investigation into claims that Meta unfairly favored its own AI assistant. The order aims to ensure fair competition in the rapidly evolving AI market, with Meta facing potential fines if found in breach of rules.

title: California dreamin', MAGA screamin'- Trump and co see red in PIO Nithya Raman's surge in LA
content: In a stunning turn of events, the Los Angeles mayoral race took a new twist as progressive Nithya Raman eclipsed Republican Spencer Pratt during vote tallying. MAGA supporters quickly raised alarm bells, claiming irregularities in the election process, despite experts pointing to California's notoriously sluggish mail-in ballot counting.

title: Walmart immigration vote: Shareholders reject report as retailer downplays visa risks
content: Walmart shareholders rejected a proposal urging a report on US immigration policy's impact on operations. The proposal cited potential workforce and supply chain risks due to policy shifts. Walmart stated it hasn't faced significant disruptions, noting its limited reliance on employment-based visas for specialized roles.

title: Gurgaon road rage: Doctor, her husband attacked during confrontation; one arrested
content: A doctor faced harassment, abuse, and threats from two men in Gurgaon's South City-II, with her car also damaged. One accused, identified as Paras Thakaran, has been arrested. The incident reportedly began with a vehicle-related argument. Police are pursuing the second suspect. The doctor's lawyer expressed dissatisfaction with the investigation and bail process.

title: FIFA World Cup opening ceremony: Time, performers & streaming
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 opening ceremony will feature three major celebrations across Mexico, Canada, and the United States, making it one of the biggest tournament launches in history. Artists including Shakira, Burna Boy, J Balvin, Tyla, Michael Bublé, and Nora Fatehi are set to perform. Fans in India can watch the ceremonies live on Unite8 Sports channels and Zee5. FIFA president Gianni Infantino called the event “a truly global celebration” uniting music, culture, and football.

title: Roger Federer car collection: Luxury cars, SUVs and Mercedes icons
content: Roger Federer still has eyes on him; he has been on the Mercedes-Benz radar since forever and is an embodiment of a luxury lifestyle. He has an array of beautiful sports cars, superior SUVs and luxury cars that beautifully depict his sophisticated personality. He's not a crazy car enthusiast and wants his garage to be appreciated by tennis fans for its performance and usability, which is why his collection is one of the most loved in tennis.

title: BTS' Dynamite becomes FIFA World Cup anthem among football fans
content: BTS’ hit song “Dynamite” has unexpectedly become part of a viral FIFA World Cup trend on TikTok and X. Football fans are using the track in South Korea-themed match edits, with several clips reportedly crossing 500,000 likes. The trend sparked hilarious reactions from ARMY members, many surprised that even non-K-pop fans know the song. Social media users say the crossover feels natural, proving BTS’ global cultural influence continues to stretch far beyond music and into sports culture.

title: Markets dumped India for AI stars. BlackRock says that’s a mistake
content: BlackRock believes India's equity market is unfairly penalized for its limited direct AI exposure and oil price sensitivity. Despite record foreign outflows and a challenging macro environment, the asset manager remains constructive on India's medium- to long-term prospects, citing strong demographics, infrastructure, and financial sector strength. They see potential for indirect AI-related opportunities and favor sectors like financials.

title: Serena and Venus Williams turn tennis dominance into a $445M business empire in 2026
content: Serena Williams and Venus Williams are two of the most successful athletes in the history of tennis, as the sisters have built a combined fortune of about $445 million. All through prize money, endorsements, business ventures and investments, and their impact beyond the WTA Tour continues to draw attention in 2026 as both legends expand their entrepreneurial empires.

title: Alpha teaser faces plagiarism row; fans spot La Femme Nikita, American Sniper links
content: The teaser of Alpha, starring Alia Bhatt and Sharvari, was unveiled on Wednesday and quickly became a talking point on social media. While many fans praised the action-packed glimpse into YRF's first female-led spy thriller, others accused the makers of borrowing scenes and ideas from international films, including the French classic La Femme Nikita and American Sniper.

title: Tamil Nadu BJP secretary quits party, to join Annamalai's 'journey'
content: Tamil Nadu BJP leader Amar Prasad has resigned from the party, following in the footsteps of former state president K Annamalai. Prasad announced his departure on social media, stating his intention to join Annamalai in his future political endeavors. This move signals a significant shift within the state's political landscape.

title: 'Mounting debts may have pushed him': Delivery executive jumps to death from 13th floor
content: A Domino's Pizza delivery worker, identified as Manav, allegedly died by suicide by jumping from the 13th floor of a building in Valsad. Preliminary information indicates severe financial distress and mounting debts may have led to the extreme step. Police are investigating the incident and recording statements from family and acquaintances.

title: From ceasefire to countdown: Frustrated Trump says Iran's time is up
content: US President Donald Trump expressed growing impatience with Iran's slow negotiation pace, warning of consequences after American forces conducted retaliatory air strikes on Iranian air-defense systems. This tit-for-tat escalation, following Iran downing a US helicopter, has jeopardized a prospective deal, with Trump resorting to strong rhetoric against Tehran's military.

title: Who will win the 2026 FIFA World Cup? Bold prediction stuns football fans
content: Artificial intelligence has predicted Spain as the favorites to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with a 14.5% chance. England and France follow closely behind. The AI model analysed team performance, player quality, and betting odds, simulating the tournament 100,000 times to determine probabilities.

title: 'Made in India' Airbus C295 takes to the skies for first test flight - Watch
content: India's first 'Made-in-India' Airbus C295 military transport aircraft successfully completed its maiden test flight in Vadodara. This marks a significant milestone as the first of 40 aircraft to be built in India by Airbus and Tata Advanced Systems Limited for the Indian Air Force, advancing the 'Make in India' vision.

title: Athletes left waiting for National Sports Awards, ministry offers no explanation
content: The concerns are not limited to former awardees. After the ministry announced the re-evaluation process, Asian Championships gold medallist decathlete Tejaswin Shankar, who has been recommended for the Arjuna Award, publicly criticised the delay. "This delay is not just de-motivating to athletes and coaches but also a sign of disrespect," he posted on X in April.

title: Virat Kohli beats SRK, Priyanka Chopra to become India's most valuable celebrity
content: Cricket star Virat Kohli has been named India's most valuable celebrity in the first-ever Fortune India-Interbrand Most Valuable Celebrities list. Kohli tops the rankings with a brand value of Rs 3,542 crore. The top 10 most valuable celebrities on the list are Virat Kohli, Shah Rukh Khan, Priyanka Chopra Jonas, MS Dhoni, Ranveer Singh, Akshay Kumar, Amitabh Bachchan, Sachin Tendulkar, Alia Bhatt and Allu Arjun.

title: 3 Indians missing, 21 rescued after vessel attacked off Oman; MEA condemns incident
content: India's External Affairs Ministry has condemned an attack on the commercial vessel Settebello off Oman's coast, with 21 of 24 Indian crew rescued and three still missing. The Palau-flagged tanker reported an engine room fire. This follows a similar incident last week where 24 Indian sailors aboard the "Marivex" were rescued by the Omani military.

title: How to protect your wealth when rupee is falling
content: Is the rupee’s fall eating into your investment returns? What should investors do to protect their portfolio in the current scenario? The first thing to understand is that fundamentally, a falling rupee does not have a direct impact on your investments.

title: 'This is a collective yagya': Modi on becoming longest serving elected PM - top quotes
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi marked becoming India's longest-serving elected Prime Minister by calling his tenure a "collective yagya" and expressing gratitude to the people. He highlighted the nation's shift towards political stability and rapid development under NDA rule, contrasting it with the "Congress Growth Rate" of the past. Modi emphasized the government's commitment to realizing the dream of a 'Viksit Bharat' for all citizens.

title: Flicks, pulls and drives on show as Rohit Sharma hits stride in nets
content: Rohit Sharma looked in fine touch during India’s optional training session in Mohali, a day after receiving CoE clearance. The veteran opener spent over an hour in the nets, while Shreyas Iyer also impressed ahead of the ODI series against Afghanistan.

title: 'They ignored him, now want Rs 45cr': Ammy Virk backs Ranveer in Don 3 row
content: Punjabi actor-singer Ammy Virk has come out in support of Ranveer Singh amid the ongoing controversy surrounding the actor's exit from Don 3. Speaking about the dispute between Ranveer and Excel Entertainment, Ammy questioned the timing of the producers' actions and suggested that the actor should not be asked to pay the reported Rs 45 crore compensation.

title: 19 TMC MPs join rebel group; Yusuf Pathan, Saayoni Ghosh, Mala Roy on list
content: A significant internal rebellion is shaking the Trinamool Congress, with 19 MPs reportedly forming a separate faction, pushing the party towards a potential split. Rebel leader Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar stated they will support the NDA but not join the BJP. MP Mahua Moitra, however, dismissed the rebellion, calling the MPs 'traitors' and citing a court verdict on party splits.

title: US stocks today: Wall Street mixed as tech stocks slide, Iran tensions weigh on sentiment
content: Indian markets mirrored global trends with a mixed opening as tech stocks faced renewed selling pressure. Investors are also wary of escalating Middle East tensions and the possibility of prolonged high interest rates. Inflation data, while meeting expectations, added to concerns, while oil prices climbed amid supply worries. Global markets also saw declines.

title: 'Most terrifying 48 hours': 3-year-old swallows metal pins baked into birthday cake
content: A toddler's third birthday celebration turned into a nightmare when he unknowingly swallowed two metal pins hidden in chocolate decorations on his cake. The pins, used as scaffolding by the bakery, were lodged in his small intestine, causing 48 hours of terror for his family before passing naturally. The incident highlights the dangers of hidden objects in children's food.

title: Channing Tatum’s quote of the day celebrates hard work, grit and perseverance
content: Channing Tatum's journey through Hollywood is a testament to relentless dedication, a mindset he showcased even in high school. He upholds the idea that real achievement comes from pouring one's heart and soul into every endeavor, be it a steamy dance number in 'Magic Mike' or an adrenaline-fueled action sequence. This unwavering work ethic is what fuels his sustained acclaim.

title: PM Modi credits ‘public maturity’ for NDA’s 12-year stable rule
content: Narendra Modi slams Congress, says “instability and turmoil” before 2014 made the country “suffer immensely”; he says benefits of a stable government is being noticed during the global crisis; PM thanks the people for letting the government board “reform express”

title: Congress calls Returning Officer’s order on Meenakshi Natarajan’s Rajya Sabha nomination ‘egregious’, set to move SC
content: Senior leaders Abhishek Manu Singhvi, who led the party’s legal arguments before the Election Commission, said the Congress had demonstrated that the Returning Officer’s decision was legally untenable and urged the ECI to reverse the decision

title: Two Indian seafarers dead, one missing after U.S. attack on ship off Oman, says sailors’ union
content: India summoned the U.S. Embassy’s Deputy Chief of Mission Jason Meeks and lodged a “strong protest” after the attack.

title: Tamil Nadu government promoted seven IAS officers without Centre’s approval, Union Ministry tells Madras High Court
content: It tells the Chief Justice’s Bench that appropriate administrative action would be taken irrespective of the dismissal of a PIL petition related to the issue

title: India summons U.S. Deputy Chief of Mission after second ship attack in three days leaves three Indian sailors missing
content: The strike on Settebello off the coast of Oman prompted the MEA to call for “immediate de-escalation of tensions and the conclusion of ongoing negotiations for a diplomatic solution so that peace and stability can return to the region”.

title: Complaint against Pantheerankavu toll plaza draws NHRC’s attention
content: Collector asked to examine allegations of staff misconduct, traffic delays, and safety deficiencies at the NH 66 facility and submit a report within eight days

title: Steps to improve functioning of Kozhikode MCH, general hospital
content: Minister visits hospitals: creation of Department of Reproductive Medicine at MCH to give a fillip to fertility treatment proposed

title: Operation Toofan: Kozhikode City police deploy K9 squad, drone unit to track down suspects
content: The frequency of field-level inspections will be increased in the coming days based on fresh intelligence inputs, say the police

title: Bengaluru seeks Centre’s nod for RRTS links to Mysuru, Tumakuru, Kolar
content: The proposal was among several key metro and transport infrastructure projects for which Mr. Shivakumar sought early approval from the Union government.

title: AIIMS recalls two syringe batches over quality concerns in a single month; probe sought
content: In a letter written to Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda, Member of Rajya Sabha Haris Beeran said that in the past month, two separate recall notices were issued within a span of three weeks for disposable 10 ml syringes with needles supplied by different manufacturers

title: Bharathiraja passes away | Mortal remains on the way to hometown; last rites with full state honours tomorrow
content: A towering personality in the film industry, Bharathiraja was fondly known as Iyakkunar Imayam (Pinnacle among Directors)

title: Supreme Court says it cannot be a mute spectator, orders Kerala to take charge of tusker amid ownership row
content: Evidence submitted in court showed that an elephant had been used for commercial and ceremonial activities despite the temporary owner giving an undertaking that he would not do so

title: Ministry recommends capping of airports for privatisation
content: The recommendation was made last week in response to queries raised by government departments represented on the Public Private Partnership Appraisal Committee

title: Teachers body seeks immediate implementation of pay revision commission, interim relief
content: Navyandhra Teachers Association leaders say that the steep rise in the prices of essential commodities, along with increasing education and healthcare expenses, has placed a heavy financial burden on the employees’ families

title: Bodies of 6 Naga men recovered in Manipur; Naga body calls for shutdown, demands removal of Deputy CM
content: Manipur Director General of Police (DGP) Mukesh Singh told The Hindu that after hectic efforts of nearly 24 hours, the mortal remains of the six missing men were located in Kangpokpi district; the deceased are believed to be among those taken hostage from Leilon Vaiphei village on May 13

title: Contesting on DMK symbol caused severe distress: Durai Vaiko
content: MDMK leader says the DMK exerted pressure to prevent his party from contesting under its own symbol in the Assembly election

title: Why Census 2027 matters for development, democracy and representation
content: Census 2027, the world’s largest count of people within a national geography, is an overdue exercise with far-reaching implications; an accurate and comprehensive count will ensure that no one is left behind and that policies, entitlements, and resources are guided by reliable data

title: Decoding SIR impact in West Bengal
content: The post-SIR polls in West Bengal saw the BJP secure a decisive victory, with AITC chief Mamata Banerjee alleging electoral manipulation. Large-scale deletions and opaque additions of electors during the SIR had a major impact on the rolls’ demographic composition; and appears to have influenced the results alongside anti-incumbency and communal polarisation

title: No pressure on families of crash victims to accept compensation: Air India
content: The daughter of former Gujarat Chief Minister Vijay Rupani, who lost his life in the crash, accused the airline of arm-twisting families to waive legal rights while accepting interim compensation

title: NDA hails Modi as longest serving continuously elected Prime Minister
content: At a meeting in Delhi, NDA leaders praise Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s leadership, adopt a resolution highlighting the government’s achievements over the past 12 years

title: EAC-PM recommends targeted splitting of seats for delimitation, shows model allowing 50% rise for all large States
content: The advisory council says it assembled a dataset on elections to LS seats from 2009 to 2024 to estimate a ‘statistical relationship’ between voter turnout, constituency size, and five compositional features of the constituency; the model results in increasing the size of the Lok Sabha to 824 seats and it is broadly in line with what the Centre had suggested in April, when it brought the Delimitation-related Bills but failed to pass them in Parliament

title: India’s reservoirs can host 102 GW of floating solar, says first national assessment
content: The report states that ground-mounted solar systems, which dominate India’s roughly 100 GW of installed solar capacity, require three to four times more area per megawatt than the panels themselves occupy

title: TMC moves Calcutta HC over CID search of party office adjacent to Mamata’s residence
content: The petition alleges the raids and search operations were conducted in the absence of the main witnesses in the case, and that important party documents were scanned

title: Anyone who wants to see Rahul as PM can join Congress: W.B. Congress chief
content: He was responding to speculation that TMC chief Mamata Banerjee may return to the Congress; rebel TMC MLA and Leader of Opposition in the State Assembly Ritabrata Banerjee insists rebels are not merging with Congress

title: Shots fired in air as ganja smugglers attack police in ASR, four nabbed
content: The smugglers, allegedly carrying ganja from Odisha, attack personnel during a vehicle check near Ramaraopalem; four held

title: Karnataka High Court frames SOP for universal accessibility for government and private establishments
content: The 44-page SOP, to be implemented in a phased manner over two years, mandates that all public buildings have to undergo accessibility audits

title: India plans 74 new land ports, three along China and six along Pakistan borders
content: The new land ports are expected to strengthen trade and seamless movement of people with the neighbouring countries

title: Yoga should be part of daily life, says Collector
content: Tourism-themed yoga session held at Pavitra Sangamam; Mylavaram MLA calls for protection of Kondapalli and Mylavaram-Mulapadu green zones

title: NDMA pushes Ministries to embed disaster resilience into planning under vision 2047
content: “Resilience is not an option; it is the foundation of a developed and prosperous India,” says NDMA member

title: Sukhendu Sekhar Ray distances himself from TMC rebel group
content: While endorsing the revolt as ‘the right thing to do’, veteran leader says he is yet to decide whether to retire after a 59-year-long political career, or join another party

title: Second Trinamool MP quits Rajya Sabha
content: Rajya Sabha MP Sushmita Dev resigned from Rajya Sabha even as speculation continues on fate of the party in Lok Sabha

title: Watch: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes in major escalation | Above the Fold | 10.06.2026
content: From Iran’s retaliation against U.S. military facilities and escalating tensions in West Asia to the Congress’s battle over Meenakshi Natarajan’s Rajya Sabha nomination, fresh turmoil within the TMC, Mamata Banerjee’s meeting with Sonia Gandhi - Top stories of the day.

title: ‘Modi is presiding over the murder of democracy in India,’ says Congress
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh accuses the Modi government of ‘dubiously inventing a milestone’ on his becoming the longest serving elected Prime Minister

title: ULBs told to submit priority projects with financing plans in 30 days
content: Official stresses structured project preparation, digital monitoring and timely implementation of urban infrastructure works

title: 4 Bangladeshi Robbers Jump In Gurugram Underpass To Run From Cops, Arrested
content: Police said that when officers moved in to arrest them, the suspects jumped into the underpass. One of them suffered fractures in both legs, while two others fractured one leg each.

title: NDA Leaders Adopt Resolution Lauding PM Modi's 12 Years In Power
content: The meeting, which brought together senior leaders from across the alliance, was chaired by BJP National President Nitin Nabin.

title: Protests Erupt In Manipur After Bodies Of 6 Naga Hostages Recovered
content: The Nagas have now declared that they will not accept the bodies of the six hostages until their charter of demands is fully addressed.

title: No Ban On Import Of Indian Mangoes, Says Nepal
content: The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare on Wednesday dismissed reports claiming that Nepal had imposed a ban or suspension on the import of mangoes from India, describing such reports as "factually incorrect and misleading".

title: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay Meets Sonia, Rahul Gandhi In Delhi
content: Vijay, who is on a three-day visit to the national capital, is expected to attend the Niti Aayog governing council meeting on Thursday.

title: Delhi Fire Deaths Rise To 23 As Bangladeshi Woman Dies During Treatment
content: A Bangladeshi woman who was injured in a fire at a hotel in south Delhi's Malviya Nagar area succumbed to her injuries during treatment on Wednesday, taking the death toll in the tragedy to 23, an official said.

title: 'Not The Person I Knew': Former Boss Of Man Behind 'Rs 370 Biryani' Remark
content: Vishwakarma said he had conducted an internal review and spoken to his team, including female colleagues. No complaints about Jangra's conduct in the workplace were found.

title: High Court Setback For Anil Ambani Aide In Money Laundering Case
content: The ED case pertains to an alleged forged bank guarantee of Rs 68.20 crore, along with endorsements and confirmations, submitted before SECI as part of the tender process.

title: Waste Management Scam In Karnataka? BJP Alleges Rs 10,000-Crore Kickback
content: The BJP's R Ashok claimed that the contract awarded to MSW Solutions Limited, linked to Ramky Group, is one of the biggest scams in the country's history.

title: "Morally Wrong": Rebel's "I Miss You Didi" Message For Mamata Banerjee
content: Asked whether she felt conflicted, Shatabdi Roy said, "I feel politically I made the right decision. But emotionally and morally I'm wrong."

title: German Model Missing For Nearly 11 Years Named In Epstein Files: Report
content: A German model missing for almost 11 years is named in the Epstein files, heightening her family's fears about her fate, German news weekly Der Spiegel reported Wednesday.

title: Casualty Of Punjab BJP's Kewal Singh Dhillon Move: Internal Cohesion
content: The incident is a symptom of dissatisfaction that is brewing within the party. The BJP's old guard appears increasingly uneasy with the pace and direction of recent changes.

title: "Never Victimised Anyone, Error In Judgement": Bill Gates On Epstein Links
content: Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates on Wednesday said that he "never victimized anyone" as he faced questioning from US lawmakers over his ties to late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

title: "No Trinamool Without Mamata Banerjee": Sagarika Ghose To NDTV
content: Trinamool's Rajya Sabha MP Sagarika Ghosh, speaking with NDTV, said despite open rebellion, "there is no Trinamool Congress without Mamata Banerjee."

title: Tamil Nadu BJP Leader's "Another DMK" Jibe After Quitting Party
content: In a nine-minute video released after his resignation, Amar Prasad Reddy detailed his contributions to the BJP since joining the party in 2013.

title: Secret routes & ghost tankers: Here's why crude hasn't hit $200 yet
content: the TOI Business Desk is a vigilant and dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant business news from around the world to readers of the Times of India . the desk ensures that TOI readers are well-informed about the ever-changing and dynamic world of commerce .

title: TMC's twin revolt: Is BJP the biggest beneficiary?
content: Aastha Jha is a writer at The Times of India, covering politics. She also writes on business, global affairs, social issues, with a focus on policy, governance and their impact on everyday lives. Her work brings together reportage and analysis, tracking developments in India and abroad while examining the broader social and economic currents shaping public discourse. Read More

title: UP RERA says carpet area is what counts, not super built-up area; here's why it matters
content: The TOI Real Estate Desk is a focused team of seasoned journalists and market watchers dedicated to decoding the ever-evolving property landscape for The Times of India readers. With a sharp eye on trends, policy shifts, and market movements, the team brings clarity to one of the most significant investment decisions in people’s lives. From expert insights on buying, selling, and investing to deep dives into infrastructure developments, home design, and sustainable living, the news here offers a comprehensive view of the real estate ecosystem. Whether you're a first-time homebuyer, a seasoned investor, or simply exploring the market, the TOI Real Estate Desk is your trusted guide to making informed property decisions. Read More

title: Crisis on all fronts: Pakistan sought Iran-US peace, faces turmoil at home
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: Peterborough Hindu temple launches judicial review over sale of site to mosque
content: a court overflow room had to be set up to accommodate the Hindus . in 1986 the city council granted a lease of unit 6 of the new England complex, Rock Road, Peterborough for 25 years . from around 2017 the council has been engaged in negotiations with the council to explore the possibility of a transfer of site to BHS .

title: UK police provides security to former Bombay HC judge and his family in London
content: the daughter of patel, a British citizen, received a death threat letter at her home on June 5, just weeks after a physical assault . the death threat was in response to a judgment passed by him in April 2024 . he upheld the claim of the 53rd dai and incumbent leader of the dawoodi Bohra community .

title: 2 soldiers killed in 'accidental blast' near LoC in Uri
content: two soldiers were taken to the army's 92 base hospital in Srinagar . they were declared dead on arrival, a spokesperson said . the explosion occurred in the Kamalkote area of the Uri sector .

title: Air India crash sole survivor Viswash still battling physical, mental and financial woes
content: the crash's sole survivor, Viswashkumar Ramesh, is still struggling with physical pain, bereavement, financial difficulties and psychological problems . he had returned to the uk on sept 15 to be with his son, Divang, now five, and also to get medical treatment . his younger brother, Ajay (35), died in the crash .

title: Mirwaiz calls PoK unrest disturbing, urges sensitive handling of protests
content: more than 20 people have been killed in clashes between police and protesters . govt there should know better than to use force to handle public grievances and demands in this manner, says cleric . the situation in Pakistan Occupied Kashmir (POK) is 'deeply disturbing'
